name: Chaos Steed
flavour: Chaos Steeds are malformed, debased parodies of the magnificent warhorses of the Empire. They are used by Possessed and other Chaos warbands.

specialRules.rulename: May not be ridden by The Possessed
specialRules.ruleAbbreviated: Cannot be ridden by Possessed models
specialRules.ruleFull: Even Chaos Steeds are skittish around the abhorrent Possessed and will not allow themselves to be ridden by such a being.

specialRules.ruleAbbreviated: Reroll failed loss of control tests
specialRules.ruleFull: The mount has been specially trained to fight on a battlefield. The rider may re-roll any failed <Tooltip>loss of control</Tooltip> tests. Only one re-roll is allowed per test.

admonitions:
Note

It is suggested that Chaos steeds should be available to all Chaos warbands, not just those that existed when these rules were originally published.
modelCaveat: Possessed warbands and Marauders with Chosen of Chaos skill only

name: Cold One
flavour: Scaly, mean and stupid, these native creatures of the New World make excellent mounts upon which to fight.

specialRules.ruleAbbreviated: Causes fear
specialRules.ruleFull: Cold Ones cause fear.

specialRules.ruleAbbreviated: Leadership test each turn or suffer stupidity
specialRules.ruleFull: Roll against the rider's Leadership each turn; if the rider fails the test then usual <Tooltip>stupidity</Tooltip> applies, otherwise move as normal.

specialRules.ruleAbbreviated: +2 armour save bonus
specialRules.ruleFull: Cold ones give an additional +1 armour save bonus (making +2 total).

caveat: Dark Elves, Lizardmen, Norse and Marauders only

name: Elven Steed
flavour: Elven steeds are graceful animals, but have a vicious temperament when called upon to fight. It is rumoured that even Dark Elves breed these fine beasts. Typically, High Elf steeds are grey and white, Wood Elf steeds are tan and white, and Dark Elf steeds are midnight black.

specialRules.ruleAbbreviated: Reroll failed loss of control tests
specialRules.ruleFull: The mount has been specially trained to fight on a battlefield. The rider may re-roll any failed <Tooltip>loss of control</Tooltip> tests. Only one reroll is allowed per test.

name: Giant Spider
flavour: The Giant Spider is the stuff of nightmares. Typically 10 to 12 feet long they are highly prized by Forest Goblins as mounts.

specialRules.ruleAbbreviated: Attacks count as Strength 4
specialRules.ruleFull: Giant Spider attacks are poisoned - attacks are considered as Strength 4, but this will not modify any armour saves.

specialRules.ruleAbbreviated: Can walk on walls, limited jumping
specialRules.ruleFull: Giant Spiders (and their riders) may walk up and down walls without making Initiative tests. They may only <Tooltip>jump</Tooltip> up to 2" across or down, but this does count as a <Tooltip>diving charge</Tooltip>. When a spider jumps, its rider must make an Initiative test; if this test is failed, something has gone wrong - roll on the <Tooltip>Whoa Boy! Table</Tooltip>. Note even if the rider has the <Tooltip>Running Dismount</Tooltip> skill, the maximum diving charge is only 2".

admonitions:
Note

Giant Spiders cannot be used in a warband that already contains Giant Wolves.

name: Giant Wolf
flavour: The giant wolf is common in most of the mountain ranges of the known world. However, catching one of these nasty, fast‑moving beasts is another thing - especially if you are a Goblin.

name: Gnoblar Fighter
flavour:
Ogres keep Gnoblars as humans keep dogs, except that Ogres don't keep their Gnoblars well. Each Ogre bites his Gnoblar's ear as a form of identification. Once an Ogre starts eating, it's difficult for him to stop. Even if they don't get eaten, the Chaos Dwarfs are likely to round them up and enslave them.

Gnoblars do not count as animals. In all other respects they are treated like War Dogs with the following additional special rules. Gnoblars count as Greenskins but do not suffer from Animosity.

specialRules.ruleAbbreviated: Dagger and sharp stuff (treat as thrown weapon with S2, range 8" and fire twice)
specialRules.ruleFull: Dagger and sharp stuff (treat as thrown weapon with S2, range 8" and fire twice)

specialRules.ruleAbbreviated: Count towards warband size but not for Rout tests
specialRules.ruleFull: Gnoblars count towards the number of warriors in a warband but are not considered for Rout tests - neither for determining the initial warband size nor as lost models.

specialRules.ruleAbbreviated: D6 roll of 1 when near another Gnoblar means they do nothing that turn
specialRules.ruleFull: Roll a D6 at the beginning of the turn for each Gnoblar within 2" of another Gnoblar and not engaged in close combat. On a result of 1 the Gnoblar starts to bicker, insult and/or bully the other Gnoblar and may do nothing else this turn.

name: Mule
flavour: Their stubbornness is legendary, but even so, these beasts of burden are occasionally ridden by Halflings, Dwarfs and even overweight clerics!

specialRules.ruleAbbreviated: Only bolt 2D6".
specialRules.ruleFull: Mules are not the fastest of mounts and only bolt 2D6".

specialRules.ruleAbbreviated: Must pass Leadership test each round or refuse to move.
specialRules.ruleFull: If a warrior is riding a mule, or is in base contact with a mule, he must make a Leadership test each round or the mule will refuse to move.

specialRules.ruleAbbreviated: Wanders randomly and bolts away from close combat without rider.
specialRules.ruleFull: With no leader or rider, a mule will wander in a random direction. Should any close combat occur within 6", it will automatically bolt directly away from the combat.

specialRules.ruleAbbreviated: Will not fight or charge; bolts when charged.
specialRules.ruleFull: Mules will not fight in combat and may not be used to charge into combat - they will simply refuse to move. If an enemy warrior charges a ridden mule, immediately roll on the <Tooltip>Whoa Boy! Table</Tooltip>. If an unridden mule is charged, it will bolt directly away from the charger.

name: Nightmare
flavour: The Vampire Counts occasionally need fell steeds to carry them about their business. Who cares if they're dead? They still have legs, don't they?

specialRules.ruleAbbreviated: Cannot run, but can charge.
specialRules.ruleFull: As an undead creature, a Nightmare may not run, but may charge as normal.

specialRules.ruleAbbreviated: Not affected by poison.
specialRules.ruleFull: Nightmares are not affected by poison.

specialRules.ruleAbbreviated: Immune to psychology; no Leadership tests; rider wound triggers Whoa Boy! Table.
specialRules.ruleFull: As an Undead creature, Nightmares are immune to psychology, never have to make Leadership tests, and will always stand still if left leaderless. However, if the rider suffers a wound, he must roll on the <Tooltip>Whoa Boy! Table</Tooltip> as normal.

modelCaveat: Vampires, Necromancer and Grave Guards only

name: Rhinox
flavour:
Below the ruined realm of the Sky Titans are the ice
fields where Rhinox herds and ice elk graze. Rhinoxen
have become a cornerstone of culture in the wild
kingdoms. To discipline these ill‑tempered beasts of
burden requires a momentous feat of strength.

specialRules.ruleAbbreviated: Adds +1 rarity roll per Strength; fail Strength test on capture or serious injury.
specialRules.ruleFull: A Hero searching for a Rhinox adds +1 to his rarity roll for each point of Strength he has. If a Rhinox was found the Hero must take a Strength test. If the test is failed he was wounded by the Rhinox while he tried to capture and tame it. Roll once on the Serious Injury table for him immediately.

specialRules.ruleAbbreviated: Causes fear.
specialRules.ruleFull: Rhinoxen are large and dangerous cave‑beasts with horns as long as a full‑grown man. They cause fear.

specialRules.ruleAbbreviated: Must charge if leadership test failed.
specialRules.ruleFull: Even Rhinoxen that have been broken by their riders have a temper shorter than a pygmy's thumb. If there is an enemy model that is an eligible target for the Rhinox rider to charge during the Declare Charges part of the Movement phase, it must immediately pass a Leadership test or declare a charge – if there is a choice of models to charge, then the controlling player may choose freely between them.

specialRules.ruleAbbreviated: Charges over 7" cause D3 impact hits at basic strength.
specialRules.ruleFull: Even a single Rhinox rider in full charge is a terrifying sight, the ground itself trembling as the cave‑beast thunders into the ranks of their foe. On any turn when a Rhinox rider charges more than 7", he causes D3 impact hits at the basic strength of the Rhinox.

specialRules.ruleAbbreviated: It treats a stunned result as knocked down instead.
specialRules.ruleFull: This creature’s head is much too thick for normal blows to penetrate. Therefore it treats a stunned result as knocked down instead.

specialRules.ruleAbbreviated: Any knocked down result the monster receives will instead cause it to lose 1 Attack until it recovers (during the Recovery phase of its next turn). Multiple knocked down results will cause it to lose multiple attacks in this way.
specialRules.ruleFull: Mighty blows will rock the monster, but most creatures lack the ability to truly knock it into the dirt. Therefore, any knocked down result the monster receives will instead cause it to lose 1 Attack until it recovers (during the Recovery phase of its next turn). Multiple knocked down results will cause it to lose multiple attacks in this way.

specialRules.ruleAbbreviated: A Rhinox adds +20 to the warband rating.
specialRules.ruleFull: A Rhinox adds +20 to the warband rating.

caveat: Ogres, Marauders, Norse and Merchant Caravans only

name: Riding/Draft Horse
flavour: Riding horses are not trained for battle and will not normally attack an enemy. However, they are useful for moving rapidly around the site of a battle.
admonitions:
Note

In the original rulebook there was an entry for horses. the only difference is that the one from the rulebook has a weapon skill of 0. This would lead to the horse being hit automatically in hand-to-hand combat and was thus changed to 1 in Border Town Burning.

name: Temple Dog
flavour:
Magical constructs, chiselled and set from enchanted stone stand immobile guarding temple grounds from intruders. On closer inspection these avatars seem indistinguishable from a normal statue, only springing into life when certain strictures have been violated on the temple grounds.

Heavy weathering on the majority of these constructs indicates they may come from an ancient time and that any such magic harnessed during their creation was lost or remains undiscovered.

Fanciful tales of hidden shrines, their priests butchered by defilers, now lie concealed in ruin. Ruins watched over by ever vigilant guardians who shall resist all attempts to settle them back into quiet stone, warding off those who dare to unravel the mystery.

specialRules.ruleAbbreviated: Causes fear.
specialRules.ruleFull: Temple Dogs are supernaturally animated celestial monuments that cause fear in other warriors.

specialRules.ruleAbbreviated: Armour save of 5+ unaffected by strength or modifiers.
specialRules.ruleFull: Because of their stone-like skin, Temple Dogs have an Armour save of 5+ that is not modified by the strength of attacks against it (or by other save modifiers).

specialRules.ruleAbbreviated: Attacks with +1 Strength when charging.
specialRules.ruleFull: Temple Dogs attack with +1 Strength when charging, due to their bulk.

specialRules.ruleAbbreviated: All attacks are considered magical.
specialRules.ruleFull: All attacks from a Temple Dog are considered to be magical in the same way as the attacks of Daemons.

specialRules.ruleAbbreviated: Not affected by poison.
specialRules.ruleFull: Temple Dogs are not affected by poison.

specialRules.ruleAbbreviated: It treats a stunned result as knocked down instead.
specialRules.ruleFull: This creature’s head is much too thick for normal blows to penetrate. Therefore it treats a stunned result as knocked down instead.

specialRules.ruleAbbreviated: Any knocked down result the monster receives will instead cause it to lose 1 Attack until it recovers (during the Recovery phase of its next turn). Multiple knocked down results will cause it to lose multiple attacks in this way.
specialRules.ruleFull: Mighty blows will rock the monster, but most creatures lack the ability to truly knock it into the dirt. Therefore, any knocked down result the monster receives will instead cause it to lose 1 Attack until it recovers (during the Recovery phase of its next turn). Multiple knocked down results will cause it to lose multiple attacks in this way.

specialRules.ruleAbbreviated: A Temple Dog adds +20 to the warband rating.
specialRules.ruleFull: A Temple Dog adds +20 to the warband rating.

caveat: Dragon Monks, Sisters of Sigmar and Priests only

name: War Boar
flavour: Large, ferocious and bad‑tempered - a perfect mount for an Orc Warlord. Orc warbands occasionally make use of these noisome beasts while exploring the ruins of Mordheim and beyond. It isn't common though, as the bigger and meaner Orcs tend to take the boars for themselves.

specialRules.ruleAbbreviated: Charges with +2 Strength (boar only).
specialRules.ruleFull: Orc war boars attack with +2S when charging, due to their bulk. Note that this applies only to the boar, not the rider.

specialRules.ruleAbbreviated: Confer +1 bonus to rider's armour save.
specialRules.ruleFull: The thick skin and matted fur of the boar makes him very hard to wound. Boars confer an additional + 1 bonus to the rider's armour save (making +2 total).

name: Wardogs
flavour: The men of the Empire have always been experts at raising ferocious bloodhounds to guard their cattle and holdings against roaming Goblins and Beastmen. A highly trained wardog is a dangerous opponent and worth its weight in gold in Mordheim.

specialRules.ruleAbbreviated: Fights like a warband member but is equipment of the Hero who bought it
specialRules.ruleFull: If you purchase a wardog, it will fight exactly like a member of your warband, though it is treated as part of the equipment of the Hero who bought it. You will need a model to represent it on a battlefield.

specialRules.ruleAbbreviated: Never gain experience, same recovery chance as Henchmen
specialRules.ruleFull: Wardogs never gain experience, and if they are put out of action they have exactly the same chance of recovering as Henchmen (ie, 1-2: Dead; 3-6: Alive).

specialRules.ruleAbbreviated: Counts towards maximum warriors in warband
specialRules.ruleFull: Wardogs count towards the maximum number of warriors allowed in your warband.<sup><Tooltip content="faq165">❓</Tooltip></sup>

admonitions:
Note

In combat the wardog behaves just like every other warrior, including counting towards rout tests. In the campaign part (shopping, upkeep, exploration), it counts as equipment so does not get counted for upkeep.

Source: Tuomas Pirinen

name: Warhorse
flavour: Warhorses are large, well‑trained horses, quite at home in battle. They are primarily used by human warbands.

specialRules.ruleAbbreviated: Rider may re-roll one failed loss of control test.
specialRules.ruleFull: The mount has been specially trained to fight on a battlefield. The rider may re‑roll any failed <Tooltip>loss of control</Tooltip> tests. Only one re‑roll is allowed per test.

specialRules.ruleAbbreviated: -1 Movement.
specialRules.ruleFull: Armour, called barding, may be purchased for a warhorse. It adds a further +1 bonus to the model's armour save, but subtracts one from its Movement. A normal horse may not wear barding.

specialRules.ruleAbbreviated: A barded warhorse is only killed on a serious injury roll of ‘1'.
specialRules.ruleFull: A barded warhorse is only killed on a serious injury roll of ‘1' if the model goes out of action.

name: Bretonnian Barding
flavour: Barding is armour for a horse in the same way that light armour is armour for a human. It covers the mount’s hide, and in some cases, its head.

specialRules.ruleAbbreviated: A barded warhorse is only killed on a serious injury roll of ‘1'.
specialRules.ruleFull: A model mounted on a barded horse receives an extra +1 to their armour save (+2 instead of the usual +1 for being mounted). In addition, a mount wearing barding will only be killed on a D6 roll of a 1 if the model is taken out of action (instead of a 1 or 2). Barding may only ever be bought for a warhorse.

admonitions:
Note

Mordheim Annual 2002, Page 103: The cost and rarity of Bretonnian barding differs radically from that listed on the trading chart. This is special Bretonnian blessed horse armour not normally available except to Bretonnian knights.

name: Buckler
flavour: Bucklers are small, round shields designed for parrying or deflecting blows. They are usually made of steel for they need to be tremendously durable to survive the brutal blows of hand-to-hand combat. Using a buckler requires great skill, but a nimble warrior can protect himself from blows which would otherwise cripple him.

specialRules.ruleAbbreviated: Can parry the first blow in each round of hand-to-hand combat.
specialRules.ruleFull: A model equipped with a buckler may <Tooltip>parry</Tooltip> the first blow in each round of hand-to-hand combat. When his opponent scores a hit, a model with a buckler may roll 1D6. If the score is greater than the highest to hit score of his opponent, the model has parried the blow, and that attack is discarded. A model may not parry attacks made with double or more its own Strength – they are simply too powerful to be stopped.

name: Chaos Armour
flavour: Chaos Armour is a suit of strangely-worked and unnatural metal. It is the mark of a Dark God's favour. While most suits of Chaos Armour are received as Gifts from an Infernal Patron, they can be acquired, though only from Chaos Dwarfs in an exclusive exchange for many captives or perhaps some impossible deed to further their interests.

specialRules.ruleAbbreviated: Gain +1 to Rarity roll per model taken out of action last battle.
specialRules.ruleFull: When searching for Chaos armour a warrior gains +1 on his <Tooltip>Rarity roll</Tooltip> for each model he took out of action in the previous battle.

specialRules.ruleAbbreviated: Cost reduced by 1 gc per Experience point the Hero has.
specialRules.ruleFull: The cost for found Chaos armour is decreased by 1 gold crown for each Experience point the Hero has.

specialRules.ruleAbbreviated: Cannot be given to another; fuses to the wearer.
specialRules.ruleFull: Chaos armour is a gift from the Dark Gods to the worthy warrior. A Hero who has successfully purchased a suit of Chaos armour will never give it away to another warband member but put it on himself immediately. Chaos armour becomes fused to the body of its wearer. It can never be removed.

specialRules.ruleAbbreviated: Does not hinder spellcasting, but cannot be combined with shield/buckler.
specialRules.ruleFull: Chaos armour does not hinder its wearer from casting spells or rituals. It can be worn by spellcasters but they cannot combine it with a shield or buckler without appropriate skills.

specialRules.ruleAbbreviated: No penalty when worn with a shield.
specialRules.ruleFull: There is no Movement penalty for combining Chaos Armour with a Shield.

caveat: Marauders of Chaos, Norse, Beastmen, Chaos Dwarfs, Possessed and Carnival of Chaos only

name: Cooking Pot Helmet
flavour: Any Master Chef worth his salt will remove his silly white hat and put on an even sillier looking cooking pot for protection when a fight is brewing. It may look incredibly stupid, but often results in an intact Halfling head after the battle.

specialRules.ruleAbbreviated: Unmodified 5+ save against being stunned, knocked down if successful.
specialRules.ruleFull: A Master Chef equipped with a cooking pot helmet has a special save of 5+ against being stunned. This save is never modified.

name: Gromril Armour
flavour: Gromril is the rarest and strongest metal known of in the Old World. Only a very few Dwarf smiths know the secret of forging gromril, and a suit of armour made from it fetches a huge price.

specialRules.ruleAbbreviated: No penalty when worn with a shield.
specialRules.ruleFull: Gromril armour<sup><Tooltip content="faq50">❓</Tooltip></sup> gives the wearer a 4+ basic save, and does not slow him down if he is also armed with a shield.

name: Heavy Armour
flavour:
Typical heavy armour is made from metal links and is called chain mail. Forging chain mail is a laborious and time consuming process, as the blacksmith must put together hundreds, sometimes thousands, of metal links. This makes chain mail expensive, but this type of armour provides excellent protection for anyone who can afford it.

There are other types of heavy armour as well, of which the best known are the steel breastplates and greaves worn by the foot knights of the Templar orders.

specialRules.ruleAbbreviated: -1 Movement when worn with a shield.
specialRules.ruleFull: A warrior that is armed with both heavy armour and a shield suffers a -1 Movement penalty

name: Helmet
flavour:
From the shining steel helmets of Bretonnian knights to the leather caps of the Skaven, all sensible warriors try to protect the most vulnerable part of their body – their head. Even the most vain fighters still use a helmet, as it can be festooned with plumes, horns and other decorations.

Helmets come in varying shapes and sizes, but their basic function remains the same.

specialRules.ruleAbbreviated: 4+ save against being stunned, treat as knocked down if successful.
specialRules.ruleFull: A model that is equipped with a helmet has a special 4+ save on a D6 against being stunned. If the save is made, treat the stunned result as knocked down instead. This save is not modified by the opponent's Strength.

name: Ithilmar Armour
flavour: Ithilmar is a silvery metal which is as light as silk and stronger than steel. Elves are experts at fashioning weapons and armour from ithilmar, and the Elven kingdom of Caledor is the only place in the world where this metal can be found.

specialRules.ruleAbbreviated: Does not slow the wearer when used with a shield.
specialRules.ruleFull: Ithilmar armour<sup><Tooltip content="faq50">❓</Tooltip></sup> gives the wearer a 5+ basic save, and does not slow him down if he is also armed with a shield.

name: Kite Shield
save: 5+ on foot, 6+ mounted (or +2 on foot, +1 mounted with armour)

specialRules.ruleAbbreviated: 5+ on foot, 6+ mounted, or +2/+1 with existing armour.
specialRules.ruleFull:
A model with a kite shield has a basic save of 5+ on a D6 while on foot, and 6+ while mounted (or, if the model is already wearing armour, as +2 on foot, and +1 save while mounted). This cannot bring a save over 1+.

(Note: If your gaming group already uses house rules for enhanced shields [ex. Ones that provide +2 AS in combat], remove Kite Shield from the equipment list.)

name: Lamellar Armour
flavour: The blacksmiths forge these heavy armours for in Cathay those noble knights protect the farmsteads. Especially among the Palace Guard of the Cathayan Emperor the plate armour is very common. The armour covers not only its wearer's torso but also the upper arm and thigh.

specialRules.ruleAbbreviated: -1 Movement when combined with shield.
specialRules.ruleFull: A warrior that is armed with both a lamellar armour and a shield suffers a –1 Movement penalty.

name: Light Armour
flavour: Light Armour encompasses a wide variety of materials from hardened leather tunics to chain shirts forged from steel. It does not offer complete protection against arrows or swords, but it is better than having nothing at all. Light armour does not inhibit movement.

specialRules.ruleAbbreviated: No penalty when worn with a shield.
specialRules.ruleFull: No penalty when worn with a shield.

name: Masterwork Heavy Armour
flavour: An heirloom brought from Tilea, where the craft of forging highly ornate and almost impenetrable heavy armour is taught.

specialRules.ruleAbbreviated: -1 Movement
specialRules.ruleFull: It grants 4+ Armour Save, but suffers from -1M even without a shield.

name: Mechanical Suit
flavour: The Curse of Stone comes to all Chaos Dwarf Sorcerers, gradually transforming them to rock from the feet up. Engineers have crafted machines which can transport their Priests as they begin to pay the price for working dark rituals.

specialRules.ruleAbbreviated: Gain +1 to Rarity roll per model taken out of action last battle.
specialRules.ruleFull: When searching for Chaos armour a warrior gains +1 on his <Tooltip>Rarity roll</Tooltip> for each model he took out of action in the previous battle.

specialRules.ruleAbbreviated: Cost reduced by 1 gc per Experience point the Hero has.
specialRules.ruleFull: The cost for found Chaos armour is decreased by 1 gold crown for each Experience point the Hero has.

specialRules.ruleAbbreviated: Cannot be given to another; fuses to the wearer.
specialRules.ruleFull: Chaos armour is a gift from the Dark Gods to the worthy warrior. A Hero who has successfully purchased a suit of Chaos armour will never give it away to another warband member but put it on himself immediately. Chaos armour becomes fused to the body of its wearer. It can never be removed.

specialRules.ruleAbbreviated: Does not hinder spellcasting, but cannot be combined with shield/buckler.
specialRules.ruleFull: Chaos armour does not hinder its wearer from casting spells or rituals. It can be worn by spellcasters but they cannot combine it with a shield or buckler without appropriate skills.

specialRules.ruleAbbreviated: +3 Movement.
specialRules.ruleFull: A Sorcerer equipped with a Mechanical suit receives +3 to Movement.

specialRules.ruleAbbreviated: No penalty when worn with a shield.
specialRules.ruleFull: There is no Movement penalty for combining Chaos Armour with a Shield.

name: Pavise
source: Ye Old Curiosity Shoppe, Mordheim Annual 2002
flavour: A pavise is a huge shield commonly used by regiments of warriors in battle to defend themselves from the arrows of their enemies. It is a weighty item and little use in a long protracted combat but excellent against shooting.

specialRules.ruleAbbreviated: counts as in cover; +1 save in close combat if charged to front.
specialRules.ruleFull: A warrior using a pavise counts as if he is in cover against missile attacks (-1 to hit). In close combat, The pavise counts as a shield (+1 armour save) but only if the warrior was charged to his front.

specialRules.ruleAbbreviated: Shields combine with armour to give +1 to the save.
specialRules.ruleFull: As explained in the Armour section, shields combine with armour to give +1 to the save.

specialRules.ruleAbbreviated: Moves at half pace.
specialRules.ruleFull: Because the Pavise is so heavy and cumbersome, the bearer moves at half pace.

name: Shield
flavour:
There are two types of shield common to the warriors of Mordheim: the first is made of wood, occasionally reinforced with metal plates. This basic type of shield, although strong, does tend to splinter, but this can sometimes save the user's life as his enemy's weapon can get trapped allowing him to strike back whilst his enemy struggles to free his weapon.

Metal shields are heavy and cumbersome, but last much longer and can take a battering. A typical Empire shield is either round or triangular, and carries the emblem of the province or city of its owner.

specialRules.ruleAbbreviated: Shields combine with armour to give +1 to the save.
specialRules.ruleFull: As explained in the Armour section, shields combine with armour to give +1 to the save.

specialRules.ruleAbbreviated: There is no movement penalty.
specialRules.ruleFull: There is no movement penalty.

name: Toughened Leathers
flavour: Expert leatherworkers are able to turn leather coats into armour (after a fashion) and those with limited funds often favor these jackets and coats as armour is very expensive. Covered with crusted salt, alcohol and other less savory materials, toughened leather is hard to penetrate and offers some protection in combat.

specialRules.ruleAbbreviated: Cannot be combined with any other armour except a helmet or buckler.
specialRules.ruleFull: Toughened leathers cannot be combined with the effects of any other armour except a helmet or buckler (so not a shield).<sup><Tooltip content="faq78">❓</Tooltip></sup>

specialRules.ruleAbbreviated: Cannot be sold back at the Trading Posts.
specialRules.ruleFull: Toughened leathers cannot be sold back at the Trading Posts; the stench alone is enough to drive away even the most desperate of buyers!

specialRules.ruleAbbreviated: Cannot cast spells like other armour
specialRules.ruleFull: Even though Toughened Leathers are purchased as miscellaneous equipment and need not be listed on a Hero’s starting armour list to be taken, they are still armour and prevent spell casting.<sup><Tooltip content="err19">✏️</Tooltip></sup>

name: Blunderbuss
flavour: A blunderbuss is a primitive blackpowder weapon, which fires a hail of lead balls, rusty bolts, bent nails, and other assorted scrap metal. It is a powerful, if erratic, weapon and takes such a long time to load that most warriors discard it after the first shot.

specialRules.ruleAbbreviated: Automatically hits all models in a 16" x 1" line.
specialRules.ruleFull: When your model fires the blunderbuss, draw a line 16" long and 1" wide in any direction from the firer (the line must be absolutely straight). Any and all models in its path are automatically hit by a Strength 3 hit.<sup><Tooltip content="faq14">❓</Tooltip></sup>

specialRules.ruleAbbreviated: May only be fired once per battle.
specialRules.ruleFull: It takes a very long time to load a blunderbuss so it may only be fired it once per battle.

name: Chaos Dwarf Blunderbuss
flavour: Chaos Dwarfs are known in battle for using a large number of these infamous blunderbusses on their infantry troops. They employ this same tactic in combat on the streets of Mordheim.

specialRules.ruleAbbreviated: Automatically hits all models in a 16" x 1" line.
specialRules.ruleFull: When your model fires the blunderbuss, draw a line 16” long and 1” wide in any direction from the firer (the line must be absolutely straight). Any and all models in its path are automatically hit by a Strength 3 hit.<sup><Tooltip content="faq14">❓</Tooltip></sup>

specialRules.ruleAbbreviated: May fire more than once; takes a full turn to reload<sup><Tooltip content="faq71">❓</Tooltip></sup>.
specialRules.ruleFull: A superior blunderbuss may be fired more than once per game; it takes a complete turn to reload<sup><Tooltip content="faq71">❓</Tooltip></sup>, so you may only fire it every other turn.

name: Double-barrelled Duelling Pistol
flavour: A natural evolution from the double-barrelled pistol, but it has proven less popular than envisaged. It had been thought that nobles would invest in them as a showpiece and put a stop to pointless duels, as who would want to shoot a rival who could shoot, miss, and then shoot again, even if you got two shots at them as well. Perhaps the designer should have put a few more hours thinking into it.

specialRules.ruleAbbreviated: +1 bonus to hit for all shots and close combat attacks
specialRules.ruleFull: A duelling pistol is built for accuracy as a skilled duellist is able to hit a coin from twenty paces. All shots and close combat attacks from a duelling pistol have a +1 bonus to hit.

specialRules.ruleAbbreviated: Takes a complete turn to reload<sup><Tooltip content="faq71">❓</Tooltip></sup>, can fire every other turn (every turn with brace)
specialRules.ruleFull: A duelling pistol takes a complete turn to reload<sup><Tooltip content="faq71">❓</Tooltip></sup>, so your model may only fire every other turn. If he has a brace of duelling pistols he may fire every turn.

specialRules.ruleAbbreviated: -2 armour save modifier
specialRules.ruleFull: Duelling pistols are even better at penetrating armour than their Strength 4 suggests. A warrior wounded by a duelling pistol must make his armour save with a -2 modifier.

specialRules.ruleAbbreviated: Can be used in close combat for +1 Attack at S4 with -2 save modifier
specialRules.ruleFull: Pistols can be used in hand-to-hand combat as well as for shooting. A model armed with a pistol and another close combat weapon gains +1 Attack<sup><Tooltip content="faq67">❓</Tooltip></sup>, which is resolved at Strength 4 with a -2 save modifier. This bonus attack can be used only once per combat. If you are firing a brace of pistols, your model can fight with 2 Attacks in the first turn of close combat. These attacks are resolved with a model's Weapon Skill like any normal close combat attack and likewise may be parried.<sup><Tooltip content="err2">✏️</Tooltip></sup> Successful hits are resolved at Strength 4 and with a -2 save modifier, regardless of the firer's Strength.

specialRules.ruleAbbreviated: Can fire one or both barrels with different effects
specialRules.ruleFull:
A double-barrelled weapon is a tricky piece of engineering, but it's a wonderful piece for combat. When firing such a weapon, the bearer must declare whether he is firing one or both barrels. If firing a single barrel, treat the shot as you would a normal weapon. However when firing both barrels, the method changes slightly:

- To hit: roll a single dice as you would normally. This allows for a narrow field of fire emanating from the weapon.
- To wound: roll for each shot individually, as each shot can wound on it's own. Treat each shot that inflicts Critical Hits separately.
- Reloading: After firing both barrels, place 2 tokens on the model. In your next shooting phase remove 1 token to represent one barrel having been reloaded. Token removal should be the last thing done in your shooting phase and you cannot shoot in the same phase as your reload.
- Firing a Brace: If you fire both guns and barrels at the same time, place 4 tokens down, 2 each of different colours (one for each pistol in the brace). Remove one token of each colour every turn.

name: Double-barrelled Handgun
flavour: Created from a request by a Nuln nobleman who had been impressed by a demonstration model, the gunsmiths slaved long and hard to replicate it until a final model was forged. By then the noble had forgotten about it and the Colleges were left with a job lot. These were given to the Gunnery School as a gift and sort of disappeared on route.

specialRules.ruleAbbreviated: Fires every other turn.
specialRules.ruleFull: A handgun takes a complete turn to reload<sup><Tooltip content="faq71">❓</Tooltip></sup>, so you may only fire it every other turn.

specialRules.ruleAbbreviated: Cannot move and fire, except to pivot or stand up.
specialRules.ruleFull: You may not move and fire a handgun in the same turn, other than to pivot on the spot to face your target or stand up.

specialRules.ruleAbbreviated: -2 armour save modifier.
specialRules.ruleFull: Handguns are even better at penetrating armour than their Strength 4 suggests. A warrior wounded by a handgun must take its armour save with a -2 modifier.

specialRules.ruleAbbreviated: Can fire one or both barrels; takes two turns to reload both.
specialRules.ruleFull:
A double-barrelled weapon is a tricky piece of engineering, but it's a wonderful piece for combat. When firing such a weapon, the bearer must declare whether he is firing one or both barrels.

- To hit: roll a single dice as you would normally. This allows for a narrow field of fire emanating from the weapon.
- To wound: roll for each shot individually, as each shot can wound on it's own. Treat each shot that inflicts Critical Hits separately.
- Reloading: After firing both barrels, place 2 tokens on the model. In your next shooting phase remove 1 token to represent one barrel having been reloaded. Token removal should be the last thing done in your shooting phase and you cannot shoot in the same phase as your reload.

name: Double-barrelled Pistol
flavour: Originally created by a forward thinking blacksmith in Ostland for a vampire hunter, the engineers in the College at Nuln picked up on the trick very quickly. The design was simple enough; it is merely a pistol with a pair of barrels and a two-part trigger, which is capable of firing one or both barrels at a time, giving it the ability to punch a hole in even the toughest armour.

specialRules.ruleAbbreviated: May only fire every other turn unless wielding a brace.
specialRules.ruleFull: A pistol takes a complete turn to reload<sup><Tooltip content="faq71">❓</Tooltip></sup>, so your model may only fire every other turn. If he has a brace of pistols he may fire every turn.

specialRules.ruleAbbreviated: Inflicts -2 modifier to armour saves.
specialRules.ruleFull: Pistols are even better at penetrating armour than their Strength 4 suggests. A warrior wounded by a pistol must make his armour save with a -2 modifier.

specialRules.ruleAbbreviated: Can be used in melee to grant +1 Attack at S4, -2 save mod.
specialRules.ruleFull: Pistols can be used in hand-to-hand combat as well as for shooting. A model armed with a pistol and another close combat weapon gains +1 Attack<sup><Tooltip content="faq67">❓</Tooltip></sup>, which is resolved at Strength 4 with a -2 save modifier. This bonus attack can be used only once per combat. If you are firing a brace of pistols, your model can fight with 2 Attacks in the first turn of close combat. These attacks are resolved with a model's Weapon Skill like any normal close combat attack and likewise may be parried.<sup><Tooltip content="err2">✏️</Tooltip></sup> Successful hits are resolved at Strength 4 and with a -2 save modifier, regardless of the firer's Strength.

specialRules.ruleAbbreviated: May fire one or both barrels; both barrels require special reloading with tokens.
specialRules.ruleFull:
A double-barrelled weapon is a tricky piece of engineering, but it's a wonderful piece for combat. When firing such a weapon, the bearer must declare whether he is firing one or both barrels.

If firing a single barrel, treat the shot as you would a normal weapon. However when firing both barrels, the method changes slightly:

- To hit: roll a single dice as you would normally. This allows for a narrow field of fire emanating from the weapon.
- To wound: roll for each shot individually, as each shot can wound on it's own. Treat each shot that inflicts Critical Hits separately.
- Reloading: After firing both barrels, place 2 tokens on the model. In your next shooting phase remove 1 token to represent one barrel having been reloaded. Token removal should be the last thing done in your shooting phase and you cannot shoot in the same phase as your reload.
- Firing a Brace: If you fire both guns and barrels at the same time, place 4 tokens down, 2 each of different colours (one for each pistol in the brace). Remove one token of each colour every turn.

name: Duelling Pistol
flavour:
A duelling pistol is a work of art, and a gunsmith labours long and hard to produce a single example. They are often carried by Imperial nobles to solve disputes over love and honour, and many a noble has died at dawn in a duel over some grievance.

Duelling pistols are prohibitively expensive weapons and common warriors rarely have them. Even if they do manage to steal or buy one, the ammunition is prohibitively expensive.

Some of the wealthiest warriors in Mordheim carry duelling pistols as status symbols, commanding great respect, admiration and envy.

specialRules.ruleAbbreviated: +1 to hit with both shooting and close combat attacks.
specialRules.ruleFull: A duelling pistol is built for accuracy as a skilled duellist is able to hit a coin from twenty paces. All shots and close combat attacks<sup><Tooltip content="faq73">❓</Tooltip></sup> from a duelling pistol have a +1 bonus to hit.<sup><Tooltip content="err3">✏️</Tooltip></sup>

specialRules.ruleAbbreviated: Fires every other turn; with a brace, may fire every turn.
specialRules.ruleFull: A duelling pistol takes a complete turn to reload<sup><Tooltip content="faq71">❓</Tooltip></sup>, so your model may only fire every other turn. If he has a brace of duelling pistols he may fire every turn.

specialRules.ruleAbbreviated: -2 armour save modifier.
specialRules.ruleFull: Duelling pistols are even better at penetrating armour than their Strength 4 suggests. A warrior wounded by a duelling pistol must make his armour save with a -2 modifier.

specialRules.ruleAbbreviated: Grants bonus attacks in close combat using pistol stats.
specialRules.ruleFull: Duelling pistols can be used in hand-to-hand combat as well as for shooting. A model armed with a duelling pistol and another close combat weapon gains +1 Attack<sup><Tooltip content="faq67">❓</Tooltip></sup>, which is resolved at Strength 4 with a -2 save modifier. This bonus attack can be used only once per combat. If you are firing a brace of duelling pistols, your model can fight with 2 Attacks in the first turn of close combat. These attacks are resolved with a model's Weapon Skill like any normal close combat attack and likewise may be parried.<sup><Tooltip content="err2">✏️</Tooltip></sup> Successful hits are resolved at Strength 4 and with a -2 save modifier, regardless of the firer's Strength.

name: Hand-held Mortar
source: Nemesis Crown Supplement / Border Town Burning supplement
flavour: The explosive power of a mortar, in a small enough package to be carried by a single man, the Hand-Held Mortar enables a warrior to launch an explosive into the midst of the enemy, sowing death and disorder.

specialRules.ruleAbbreviated: May only fire every other turn due to reloading.
specialRules.ruleFull: A Hand-held Mortar takes a complete turn to reload<sup><Tooltip content="faq71">❓</Tooltip></sup>, so you may only fire it every other turn.

specialRules.ruleAbbreviated: Cannot move and fire in the same turn, except pivot or stand up.
specialRules.ruleFull: You may not move and fire a Hand-held Mortar in the same turn, other than to pivot on the spot to face your target or stand up.

specialRules.ruleAbbreviated: Armour saves suffer a -2 modifier when wounded.
specialRules.ruleFull: Hand-held Mortar are even better at penetrating armour than their Strength 4 suggests. A warrior wounded by a Hand-held Mortar must take its armour save with a -2 modifier.

specialRules.ruleAbbreviated: Missed shots land 2D6" in a random direction.
specialRules.ruleFull: If the warrior misses his roll to hit, the shot will land 2D6” in a random direction (determined using a Warhammer directional die, using the “clockface method” of scattering, or whatever other method the players can agree to).

specialRules.ruleAbbreviated: Subject to optional Blackpowder Weapons rules; non-BOOM results jam the weapon.
specialRules.ruleFull: The Hand-held Mortar is always Experimental: subject to the <Tooltip>optional Blackpowder Weapons rules</Tooltip> from the Mordheim rulebook, even if they are not normally used in your campaign. On any result other than “BOOM!”, the weapon has jammed or run out of loaded barrels and must be reloaded.

specialRules.ruleAbbreviated: Target and all models within 1½” take a S4 hit.
specialRules.ruleFull: After determining the final landing spot, the explosion created by the bomb will cover a small area. The target and any models within 1 ½” of him each take a single S4 hit from the blast.

caveat: Gunnery School of Nuln and Maneaters only

name: Handgun
flavour: A handgun is a simple firearm. The quality of construction varies ranging from the crude wooden ‘hakbuts' of the artillery school of Nuln, to the more sophisticated Dwarf firearms that have levers and springs which hold the burning match, and triggers which release the firing mechanism and fire the gun. Handguns are not terribly reliable weapons: the gun barrel occasionally tends to explode violently or the powder fails to ignite. But the weapon has a great range and tremendous penetrating power, making a mockery of even the thickest armour. In Mordheim, handguns are rare and expensive, but a warband which can boast such a weapon will command respect from all its rivals.

specialRules.ruleAbbreviated: May only fire every other turn due to reloading.
specialRules.ruleFull: A handgun takes a complete turn to reload<sup><Tooltip content="faq71">❓</Tooltip></sup>, so you may only fire it every other turn.

specialRules.ruleAbbreviated: Cannot move and fire in the same turn, except pivot or stand up.
specialRules.ruleFull: You may not move and fire a handgun in the same turn, other than to pivot on the spot to face your target or stand up.

specialRules.ruleAbbreviated: Armour saves suffer a -2 modifier when wounded.
specialRules.ruleFull: Handguns are even better at penetrating armour than their Strength 4 suggests. A warrior wounded by a handgun must take its armour save with a -2 modifier.

name: Hersten-Wenkler Pigeon Bombs
flavour: When the full potential of gunpowder was realized in the Empire, it became only a matter of time before some enterprising engineers combined explosives and small animals. After initial failures with attempts to use rats, bats, and dogs, promising results were realized with pigeons. While not completely accurate due to having something of a mind of their own, pigeons are able to get to even distant targets quickly, and are very hard to prevent from reaching their target once they have been launched. Once it gets there, pigeon's small metal harness will fall away, freeing the bomb to ravage a small area below, while the pigeon returns home.

specialRules.ruleAbbreviated: Cannot move and fire in the same turn, except pivot or stand up.
specialRules.ruleFull: You may not move and fire a Pigeon Bomb in the same turn, other than to pivot on the spot to face your target or stand up from knocked down.

specialRules.ruleAbbreviated: As per the Hand-held Mortar's Explosive Radius rule.
specialRules.ruleFull: If the Pigeon Bomb lands on target, use the Explosive Radius rule under the Hand-Held Mortar above to determine the area of effect.

specialRules.ruleAbbreviated: Roll a D6: 5-6 hits, 2-4 fizzles, 1 explodes in the user's hands.
specialRules.ruleFull: When launching a pigeon bomb, do not use the BS of the warrior. Instead, roll a D6: on a 5-6, the a pigeon bomb hits its target; on a 2-4 the fuse wasn't cut properly and the pigeon explodes harmlessly in the air before reaching its target; on a result of 1, something has gone disastrously wrong and the pigeon explodes in the hero's hands… he and everyone within 1 ½” takes a S4 hit.

specialRules.ruleAbbreviated: Once purchased, the Hero has enough for the whole campaign.
specialRules.ruleFull: Once a Hero buys pigeon bombs, he has enough for the full game, and his supply gets replenished at the start of each new game.

name: Hunting Rifle
flavour: Hochland is a province famed for its hunters, and the preferred weapon of its nobility when they go hunting is a long-ranged rifle. They are extremely rare and precious weapons, and only the most experienced weaponsmiths are capable of manufacturing them.

specialRules.ruleAbbreviated: Cannot move and fire in the same turn, except to pivot or stand up.
specialRules.ruleFull: You may not move and fire a Hochland long rifle in the same turn, other than to pivot on the spot to face your target or stand up from knocked down.

specialRules.ruleAbbreviated: Can only fire every other turn due to reloading.
specialRules.ruleFull: A Hochland long rifle takes a complete turn to reload<sup><Tooltip content="faq71">❓</Tooltip></sup>, so you may only fire it every other turn.

specialRules.ruleAbbreviated: Can target any visible enemy, not just the closest.
specialRules.ruleFull: A model armed with a Hochland long rifle can target any enemy model in sight, not just the closest one.

specialRules.ruleAbbreviated: Wounded targets suffer a -2 armour save modifier.
specialRules.ruleFull: Hochland long rifles are even better at penetrating armour than their Strength 4 suggests. A warrior wounded by a long rifle must make his armour save with a -2 modifier.

name: Ostlander Double-barrelled Hunting Rifle
source: Town Cryer #11, revised in Mordheim Annual 2002
flavour: Knowing Ostlanders' penchant for impressive weaponry (and ready willingness to spend excessive amounts of money) a weaponsmith from Hochland decided to weld two barrels together on a pistol and sell it for twice the price. The warband was so impressed with their new weapon that they asked him to do the same to their hunting rifle. Since then, the weaponsmith has been flooded with orders from some of the most powerful warbands in Mordheim.

specialRules.ruleAbbreviated: A double-barrelled gun (of any sort) is treated exactly like a normal version with one exception. Any enemy is hit by two blasts rather than one (ie, a pistol causes two S4 hits rather than one for each successful hit). However, each barrel takes a full turn to reload<sup><Tooltip content="faq71">❓</Tooltip></sup> (although if you reload only one barrel you can fire it like a normal pistol/rifle). If you own a brace of double-barreled pistols you may fire them every other round (rather than every round like a normal brace).
specialRules.ruleFull: A double-barrelled gun (of any sort) is treated exactly like a normal version with one exception. Any enemy is hit by two blasts rather than one (ie, a pistol causes two S4 hits rather than one for each successful hit). However, each barrel takes a full turn to reload<sup><Tooltip content="faq71">❓</Tooltip></sup> (although if you reload only one barrel you can fire it like a normal pistol/rifle). If you own a brace of double-barreled pistols you may fire them every other round (rather than every round like a normal brace).

name: Ostlander Double-barrelled Pistol
source: Town Cryer #11, revised in Mordheim Annual 2002
flavour: Knowing Ostlanders' penchant for impressive weaponry (and ready willingness to spend excessive amounts of money) a weaponsmith from Hochland decided to weld two barrels together on a pistol and sell it for twice the price. The warband was so impressed with their new weapon that they asked him to do the same to their hunting rifle. Since then, the weaponsmith has been flooded with orders from some of the most powerful warbands in Mordheim.

specialRules.ruleAbbreviated: A double-barrelled gun (of any sort) is treated exactly like a normal version with one exception. Any enemy is hit by two blasts rather than one (ie, a pistol causes two S4 hits rather than one for each successful hit). However, each barrel takes a full turn to reload<sup><Tooltip content="faq71">❓</Tooltip></sup> (although if you reload only one barrel you can fire it like a normal pistol/rifle). If you own a brace of double-barreled pistols you may fire them every other round (rather than every round like a normal brace).
specialRules.ruleFull: A double-barrelled gun (of any sort) is treated exactly like a normal version with one exception. Any enemy is hit by two blasts rather than one (ie, a pistol causes two S4 hits rather than one for each successful hit). However, each barrel takes a full turn to reload<sup><Tooltip content="faq71">❓</Tooltip></sup> (although if you reload only one barrel you can fire it like a normal pistol/rifle). If you own a brace of double-barreled pistols you may fire them every other round (rather than every round like a normal brace).

name: Pistol
flavour: A pistol is a small, simple blackpowder weapon fired by a spring mechanism. Most pistols are expensive, unreliable, and poorly constructed.

specialRules.ruleAbbreviated: Can only fire every other turn unless using a brace.
specialRules.ruleFull: A pistol takes a whole turn to reload<sup><Tooltip content="faq71">❓</Tooltip></sup>, so you may only fire every other turn. If you have a brace of pistols (ie, two) you may fire every turn.

specialRules.ruleAbbreviated: Wounded targets suffer a -2 armour save modifier.
specialRules.ruleFull: Pistols are even better at penetrating armour than their Strength 4 suggests. A warrior wounded by a pistol must make his armour save with a -2 modifier.

specialRules.ruleAbbreviated: Can be used in melee for +1 Attack; brace grants 2 Attacks first round.
specialRules.ruleFull: Pistols can be used in hand-to-hand combat as well as for shooting. A model armed with a pistol and another close combat weapon gains +1 Attack<sup><Tooltip content="faq67">❓</Tooltip></sup>, which is resolved at Strength 4 with a -2 save modifier. This bonus attack can be used only once per combat. If you are firing a brace of pistols, your model can fight with 2 Attacks in the first turn of close combat. These attacks are resolved with a model's Weapon Skill like any normal close combat attack and likewise may be parried.<sup><Tooltip content="err2">✏️</Tooltip></sup> Successful hits are resolved at Strength 4 and with a -2 save modifier, regardless of the firer's Strength.

name: Repeater Handgun
flavour: The next step in the evolution of multi-barrelled weapons, a Repeater Handgun mounts a number of handgun barrels around a rotating cylinder, each one firing in turn. Prone to misfiring or other malfunction, Repeater Handguns are still highly sought after items, as they will launch a veritable storm of lead at the enemy if they don't break down.

specialRules.ruleAbbreviated: Wounded targets suffer -2 to their armour save.
specialRules.ruleFull: Handguns are even better at penetrating armour than their Strength 4 suggests. A warrior wounded by a handgun must take its armour save with a -2 modifier.

specialRules.ruleAbbreviated: Cannot move and shoot in the same turn.
specialRules.ruleFull: You may not move and fire a handgun in the same turn, other than to pivot on the spot to face your target or stand up.

specialRules.ruleAbbreviated: May fire up to 3 shots; -1 to hit on additional shots.
specialRules.ruleFull: The Repeater Handgun may fire up to three shots; if more than one shot is made the to hit roll is at -1. Resolve each shot individually, you may choose to shot later shots at a different target, but they must be within 3" of the previous target. Ordinary targeting restrictions apply to shots as per Mordheim rulebook.

specialRules.ruleAbbreviated: Always uses optional Blackpowder Weapons rules.
specialRules.ruleFull: The Repeater Handgun is always Experimental: subject to the <Tooltip>optional Blackpowder Weapons rules</Tooltip> from the Mordheim rulebook, even if they are not normally used in your campaign. On any result other than “BOOM!”, the weapon has jammed or run out of loaded barrels and must be reloaded.

specialRules.ruleAbbreviated: Must do nothing for one full turn to reload.<sup><Tooltip content="faq71">❓</Tooltip></sup>
specialRules.ruleFull: To reload a Repeater Handgun takes a Slow Reload long time and a bit of focus. The warrior must do nothing (no moving, no shooting, no fighting in close combat, etc.) for a complete turn in order to reload the weapon.

name: Repeater Pistol
flavour: It wasn't long after the invention of the repeater handgun that the same principle was being used on pistols. Still prone to misfiring or other malfunction, they have found a place in gunnery bands armouries. Stories of calm instructors gunning down hordes of foes within feet of themselves are legendary and in all likelihood untrue. But it just could happen...

specialRules.ruleAbbreviated: Wounded targets suffer -2 to their armour save.
specialRules.ruleFull: Pistols are even better at penetrating armour than their Strength 4 suggests. A warrior wounded by a handgun must take its armour save with a -2 modifier.

specialRules.ruleAbbreviated: May fire up to 3 shots; -1 to hit on additional shots.
specialRules.ruleFull: The Repeater Pistol may fire up to three shots; if more than one shot is made the to hit roll is at -1. Resolve each shot individually, you may choose to shot later shots at a different target, but they must be within 3" of the previous target. Ordinary targeting restrictions apply to shots as per Mordheim rulebook.

specialRules.ruleAbbreviated: Always uses optional Blackpowder Weapons rules.
specialRules.ruleFull: The Repeater Pistol is always Experimental: subject to the <Tooltip>optional Blackpowder Weapons rules</Tooltip> from the Mordheim rulebook, even if they are not normally used in your campaign. On any result other than “BOOM!”, the weapon has jammed or run out of loaded barrels and must be reloaded.

specialRules.ruleAbbreviated: Can always fire one shot; needs 1 turn to reload<sup><Tooltip content="faq71">❓</Tooltip></sup> for multi-shot.
specialRules.ruleFull: The basic design of the pistol and number of barrels allows quick reloading, the pistol will always be able to fire at least one shot. After shooting more than one shot in a single turn the model must spend a complete shooting phase without shooting and without being in combat, before firing multiple shots (see Fire Thrice, above) may be taken again.

specialRules.ruleAbbreviated: Only usable in first round of combat; too delicate to use as a club.
specialRules.ruleFull: The Repeater Pistol may be used as a normal pistol in the first round of combat. After that it doesn't count as an additional hand weapon as it is far too delicate to risk in such a crude fashion. Its owner will not willingly discard it in a fight and so must fight on without the use of an additional hand weapon.

name: Swivel Gun
flavour: Pirate Gunners sometimes construct and carry into battle a smaller and lighter version of the real swivel cannons normally attached to pivoting mounts on the ship's railing or sides. Though smaller than regular cannons, swivel guns are larger than normal handguns, so big that they must be held up with the support of wooden support. They are cumbersome, and prone to failure due to imperfect castings or poorly mixed blackpowder, but most gunners agree that they make up for it in sheer power.

specialRules.ruleAbbreviated: May only fire every other turn due to reloading.
specialRules.ruleFull: A handgun takes a complete turn to reload<sup><Tooltip content="faq71">❓</Tooltip></sup>, so you may only fire it every other turn.

specialRules.ruleAbbreviated: Cannot move and fire in the same turn, except pivot or stand up.
specialRules.ruleFull: You may not move and fire a handgun in the same turn, other than to pivot on the spot to face your target or stand up.

specialRules.ruleAbbreviated: A Pirate Warband may have only one Swivel Gun  at a time.
specialRules.ruleFull: A Pirate Warband may have only one Swivel Gun  at a time, due to the time it takes the ship’s Master Gunner to construct one.

specialRules.ruleAbbreviated: User suffers -1 M and -1 I; cannot move and fire, or fire twice.
specialRules.ruleFull: The user is at –1 Initiative and –1 Movement throughout the battle. Also, Swivel Guns may never be fired twice per turn, or fired if the user moved, no matter what Skills the user may have.

specialRules.ruleAbbreviated: Always uses optional Blackpowder Weapons rules.
specialRules.ruleFull: The normally optional rules for Blackpowder weapons are always in effect for Swivel Guns, due to unpredictable nature of the local materials used in their construction.

specialRules.ruleAbbreviated: Ammo must be bought for each game and declared before firing.
specialRules.ruleFull:
Swivel Guns use nonstandard ammunition types, which must be bought for each game. Each type only lasts one game, so if it is used in a game it cannot be used again until another supply is bought. Before firing, the Gunner must declare which type is being used, if he has more than one type available in the game.

Ball Shot

A Swivel Gun firing these heavy lead balls can stop even a charging Ogre dead in his tracks!

Cost: 5 gc

Maximum Range: 36";
Strength: 5;
Special Rule: Armour Save -2, Concussion.

Concussion: The impact of the heavy lead projectile is enough to rattle even the hardiest warrior. Treat any resulting Injury Rolls of 2-4 as a Stunned result.

Chain Shot

These lengths of chain and linked metal don't cause as much damage, but can entangle an enemy model and bring him to his knees.

Cost: 2 gc

Maximum Range: 24";
Strength: 4;
Special Rule: Armour Save -1, All Wrapped Up!

All Wrapped Up! Enemy hit by Chain Shot which are not wounded are Knocked Down on a roll of 4+, even if they normally can never be Knocked Down.

Grape Shot

Very small pellets, rocks, metal scrap, even rock salt are poured into the barrel from prepared canisters, producing a cloud of shrapnel when fired.

Cost: 2 gc

Maximum Range: 24";
Strength: 3;
Special Rule: It's Everywhere!

It's Everywhere! If a hit is scored, D6 other enemy models within 4" of the target and also in Line of Sight will automatically take a single hit. If the original target was in the open, no hits can be applied to models in cover though (only if the original target was in cover can hits go to models in cover as well). The closest enemy model to the target must take the first hit, then the next closest, and so on. Models in Hiding will also count towards being close to the target, and can be hit as well. There is no Armour Save modifier from Grape Shot hits. Pirates know to duck out of the way when they hear a Swivel Gun going off, and thus are never hit by friendly Grape Shot.

name: Warplock pistol
flavour: Warplock pistols are terrifying weapons, testimony to the mad genius of Clan Skryre engineers. Warplock pistols shoot ammunition made of magically treated warpstone and wounds caused by warplock pistols are horrible to behold and often cause infections.

specialRules.ruleAbbreviated: Can only fire every other turn unless using a brace.
specialRules.ruleFull: A pistol takes a whole turn to reload<sup><Tooltip content="faq71">❓</Tooltip></sup>, so you may only fire every other turn. If you have a brace of pistols (ie, two) you may fire every turn.

specialRules.ruleAbbreviated: Wounded targets suffer a -2 armour save modifier.
specialRules.ruleFull: Warplock Pistols are even better at penetrating armour than their Strength 5 suggests. A warrior wounded by a pistol must make his armour save with a -3 modifier.

specialRules.ruleAbbreviated: Can be used in close combat.
specialRules.ruleFull: Pistols can be used in hand-to-hand combat as well as for shooting. A model armed with a pistol and another close combat weapon gains +1 Attack<sup><Tooltip content="faq67">❓</Tooltip></sup>, which is resolved at Strength 5 with a -3 save modifier. This bonus attack can be used only once per combat. If you are firing a brace of pistols, your model can fight with 2 Attacks in the first turn of close combat. These attacks are resolved with a model’s Weapon Skill like any normal close combat attack and likewise may be parried. Successful hits are resolved at Strength 5 and with a -3 save modifier, regardless of the firer’s Strength.<sup><Tooltip content="faq64">❓</Tooltip></sup>

name: Axe
flavour:
The axe is the traditional weapon of Empire woodsmen, and is also used as a weapon in poorer rural areas. Axes have a heavy blade and, if swung by a strong man, can cause a lot of damage. The blade of an axe can easily cut through armour, though it requires considerable strength from the wielder.

Of all the warriors in the Old World, Dwarfs are the most adept at making axes. Their axes are invaluable to the warriors of the Old World and are some of the most sought after weapons.

specialRules.ruleAbbreviated: An axe has an extra save modifier of -1
specialRules.ruleFull: An axe has an extra save modifier of -1, so a model with Strength 4 using an axe has a -2 save modifier when he hits an opponent in hand-to-hand combat.

name: Ball and Chain
flavour: This is a huge iron ball with a chain attached, used by the dreaded Night Goblin Fanatics to deal out whirling death. Enormously heavy, it can only be used when combined with Mad Cap Mushrooms.

specialRules.ruleAbbreviated: No armour saves are allowed, causes 1D3 wounds instead of 1
specialRules.ruleFull: Because the Ball and Chain is so heavy, normal armour does very little to protect against it. No armour saves are allowed against wounds caused by a Ball and Chain. In addition, any hit from a Ball and Chain is very likely to take off someone's head (or at least break some ribs!). Therefore, any hit that successfully wounds will do 1D3 wounds instead of 1.

specialRules.ruleAbbreviated: The first turn, moves 2D6". In his subsequent Movement phases, roll a D6 to determine what the model does
specialRules.ruleFull:
The only way to wield a Ball and Chain is to swing it around in large circles, using your body as a counter-weight. Unfortunately this is not a very controllable fighting style, and as soon as he starts swinging his Ball and Chain, a warrior starts to lose control. The first turn he starts swinging the Ball and Chain, the model is moved 2D6" in a direction nominated by the controlling player. In his subsequent Movement phases, roll a D6 to determine what the model does:

  D6  Result
1The model trips and strangles himself with the chain. The model is taken out of action. When rolling for Injury after the game, a roll of 1-3 means the model is out permanently, instead of the normal 1-2.
2-5The model moves 2D6" in a direction nominated by the controlling player.
6The model moves 2D6" in a random direction. If the player owns a Scatter dice (available from Games Workshop stores), roll that to determine direction. If not, then roll a D6: 1 – Straight Forward, 2-3 – Right, 4-5 – Left, 6 – Straight Back.

If the Ball and Chain wielding model moves into contact with another model (friend or foe), he counts as charging into close combat, and will engage in close combat until his next Movement phase. Opponents wishing to attack a Ball and Chain wielding model suffer a To Hit penalty of -1, as they must dodge the whirling ball to get close enough to strike. The Ball and Chain wielder cannot be held in close combat and will automatically move even if he starts the Movement phase in base contact with another model. If the model moves into contact with a building, wall, or other obstruction, he is automatically taken out of action. In addition, a ball and chain wielding Goblin is much too busy trying to control the spinning weapon to worry about what others are saying about him behind his back, so ignores the special rules for Animosity.

specialRules.ruleAbbreviated: A model equipped with one may carry no other weapons or equipment, requires Mad Cap Mushrooms.
specialRules.ruleFull: Because the Ball and Chain is so heavy, a model equipped with one may carry no other weapons or equipment. In addition, only a model under the influence of <Tooltip>Mad Cap Mushrooms</Tooltip> has the strength to wield a ball and chain.

specialRules.ruleAbbreviated: Must roll for Injury after battle as if taken out of action.
specialRules.ruleFull: The great weight of the Ball and Chain can easily tear ligaments or pull a wielder's arms out of their sockets. While someone under the influence of Mad Cap Mushrooms will not notice such effects, when the drug wears off he will be in great pain. To represent this, at the end of the battle the controlling player must roll for Injury for each model that used a Ball and Chain, just as if the model had been taken out of action. If the model was actually taken out of action normally, just roll once for Injury – there is no need to make a second roll.

name: Barbed Whip
flavour: Originally used for taming the wild Chaos Hounds the barbed whips have proven effective in combat also.

specialRules.ruleAbbreviated: When the wielder charges, they gain +1 Attack for that turn. When charged, they gain +1 Attack against the charger which strikes first.
specialRules.ruleFull: When the wielder charges they gain +1A for that turn. This bonus attack is added after any other modifications. When the wielder is charged they gain +1A that they may only use against the charger. This additional attack will 'strike first'. If the wielder is simultaneously charged by two or more opponents they will still only receive a total of +1A. If the wielder is using two whips at the same time then they get +1A for the additional hand weapon, but only the first whip gets the whipcrack +1A.<sup><Tooltip content="err49">✏️</Tooltip></sup>

specialRules.ruleAbbreviated: A model attacked by a barbed whip may not parry.
specialRules.ruleFull: A model attacked by a barbed whip may not <Tooltip>parry</Tooltip> with a sword or buckler.

specialRules.ruleAbbreviated: As long as he is not involved in close combat all Warhounds of Chaos within 4" gain +1 attack.
specialRules.ruleFull: The Hero may use his whip to make the Warhounds charge wildly. As long as he is not involved in close combat all Warhounds of Chaos within 4" gain +1 attack.

caveat: One Marauders of Chaos Hero only

name: Beastlash
flavour: The Beastmaster make good use of their whips to goad their hounds and creatures into combat.

specialRules.ruleAbbreviated: Animals charging or being charged by the wielder must pass a Fear test.
specialRules.ruleFull: The Beastmaster wielding a Beastlash causes Fear in animals, any animal charged or wishing to charge a Beastmaster with one of these weapons must first take a <Tooltip>Fear</Tooltip> test as mentioned in the psychology section of the Mordheim rules.

specialRules.ruleAbbreviated: A model attacked by a barbed whip may not parry.
specialRules.ruleFull: The Beastlash is a flexible weapon and the Beastmaster use it with great expertise. Attempts to parry its strikes are futile. A model attacked by a Beastlash may not make <Tooltip>parries</Tooltip> with swords or bucklers.

specialRules.ruleAbbreviated: When the wielder charges, they gain +1 Attack for that turn. When charged, they gain +1 Attack against the charger which strikes first.
specialRules.ruleFull: When the wielder charges they gain +1A for that turn. This bonus attack is added after any other modifications. When the wielder is charged they gain +1A that they may only use against the charger. This additional attack will ‘strike first'. If the wielder is simultaneously charged by two or more opponents they will still only receive a total of +1A. If the wielder is using two whips at the same time then they get +1A for the additional hand weapon, but only the first whip gets the whipcrack +1A.

name: Bec de Corbin
flavour: A Tilean invention, Bec de Corbin is a polearm that combines the best aspects of a Halberd, spear and warhammer.

specialRules.ruleAbbreviated: It requires two hands to use.
specialRules.ruleFull: A model armed with a Bec de Corbin may not use a shield, buckler or additional weapon in close combat. If the model has a shield he still gets a +1 bonus to his armour save against shooting.

specialRules.ruleAbbreviated: The model armed with Bec de Corbin strikes in initiative order when charged instead of going last.
specialRules.ruleFull: The model armed with Bec de Corbin strikes in initiative order when charged instead of going last.

specialRules.ruleAbbreviated: When rolling for Injuries, 2-4 is treated as stunned when rolling on the Injury chart. Dwarfs are immune to this effect.
specialRules.ruleFull: When rolling for Injuries, 2-4 is treated as stunned when rolling on the Injury chart. Dwarfs are immune to this effect.

name: Boat Hook
flavour: These are normally used to pull in ropes or other objects from the water, but their long reach and wicked metal catches makes them also useful in combat.

specialRules.ruleAbbreviated: The Boat Hook Strike First in Close Combat
specialRules.ruleFull: Boat Hooks are used in Close Combat. They allow the user to Strike First in the first round of any close combat, no matter which model charged, but require both hands to use.

specialRules.ruleAbbreviated: Models using a Boat Hook in combat cannot use any other weapons
specialRules.ruleFull: Models using a Boat Hook in combat cannot use any other weapons, or gain benefit from a shield or buckler, while in close combat.

name: Boss Pole
flavour: Some influential Goblins carry badges of office, usually taking the form of long wooden poles with an icon or sharp blade on the end. This allows the hero and any Goblin henchmen within 6" to ignore animosity. Additionally, the Boss Pole acts as a spear in close combat.

specialRules.ruleAbbreviated: Strikes first when charged
specialRules.ruleFull: A warrior with a spear strikes first, even if charged. Note that this only applies in the first turn of hand-to-hand combat.

specialRules.ruleAbbreviated: Cannot use second weapon
specialRules.ruleFull: A warrior with a spear may only use a shield or a buckler in his other hand. He may not use a second weapon.<sup><Tooltip content="err42">✏️</Tooltip></sup>

specialRules.ruleAbbreviated: +1 Strength when charging mounted
specialRules.ruleFull: If using the optional rules for mounted models, a mounted warrior armed with a spear receives a +1 Strength bonus when he charges. This bonus only applies for that turn.

specialRules.ruleAbbreviated: Allows nearby Goblins to ignore Animosity
specialRules.ruleFull: Heroes and henchmen within 6" ignore <Tooltip>Animosity</Tooltip>.

name: Brass Knuckles
source: Ye Old Curiosity Shoppe, Town Cryer 7, this was removed in the Mordheim Annual 2002
flavour: Brass knuckles are a weapon commonly used by streets thugs and robbers who are all too common an infestation in the mighty cities of the Empire. Easily secreted, they are used in pairs and while cumbersome to use in a straight fight, can cause crippling blows to an opponent with a single well landed punch.

specialRules.ruleAbbreviated: Grants extra Attack when used in pairs
specialRules.ruleFull: Brass knuckles are used in pairs and the warrior fighting with them gains an extra Attack. He may not use any other weapons or items in his hands while doing this however. He is free to swap his brass knuckles for other weapons and items during the battle but may not do this if in combat.

specialRules.ruleAbbreviated: -2 Initiative in close combat
specialRules.ruleFull: Brass knuckles are difficult to use due to the fact that they offer little in the way of range and a warrior must get close up to his opponent before he can strike. For this reason a warrior using brass knuckles suffers a -2 to Initiative in close combat.

name: Brazier Iron
source: Ye Old Curiosity Shoppe, Mordheim Annual 2002
flavour: The brazier Iron is a weapon commonly used by witch hunters. It consists of a long heft topped by an iron cup filled with burning hot coals. In combat, the weapon takes on an eldritch quality as the burning embers sear the air as it is swung, opponents sent reeling in flaming agony as they are set on fire.

specialRules.ruleAbbreviated: Requires two hands to wield
specialRules.ruleFull: A warrior armed with a brazier staff requires two hands to wield it effectively and so may not use a shield with it or another hand weapon or buckler in close combat. The warrior may still use a shield for the purposes of missile fire though.

specialRules.ruleAbbreviated: Chance to set target on fire
specialRules.ruleFull: The burning brazier of coals atop the staff is deadly, capable of setting an opponent ablaze with even the slightest glancing blow. Whenever you score a successful hit with the brazier staff roll a D6. If you roll a 5+ the victim is set on fire. If the warrior survives the attack they must score a 4+ in the recovery phase or they will suffer a strength 4 hit each turn they are on fire and will be unable to do anything other than move. Other warriors from the same warband may help to put the warrior out if they wish. They must move into base-to-base contact and score a 4+ in the recovery phase.

specialRules.ruleAbbreviated: Cannot use second weapon or buckler
specialRules.ruleFull: A model with a Broadsword may not use a second weapon or buckler in his other hand because it requires all his skill to wield it. He may carry a shield or a kite shield as normal though.

specialRules.ruleAbbreviated: Always strikes last
specialRules.ruleFull: Broadswords are so heavy that the model using them always strikes last, even when charging. Just like a Double-Handed weapon, learning the skill 'Strongman' negates 'Strike Last'.

name: Cat O' Nine Tails
flavour: Order is often maintained aboard the ship with the threat of the lash. In battle the long barbed whip of the Cat is also seen, but this time dealing out punishment to the enemy!

specialRules.ruleAbbreviated: Attacks cannot be parried
specialRules.ruleFull: Like the Steel Whips of the Sisterhood, Cats cannot be <Tooltip>parried</Tooltip> by swords or bucklers.

specialRules.ruleAbbreviated: Enemy gets +1 to armour save
specialRules.ruleFull: Since they are made of rope and not steel, they give the enemy model a +1 to his armour save (6+ for no armour), like a hit from a fist or dagger.

specialRules.ruleAbbreviated: +1A when charging or being charged
specialRules.ruleFull: When the wielder charges they gain +1A for that turn. This bonus attack is added after any other modifications. When the wielder is charged they gain +1A that they may only use against the charger. This additional attack will 'strike first'. If the wielder is simultaneously charged by two or more opponents they will still only receive a total of +1A. If the wielder is using two whips at the same time then they get +1A for the additional hand weapon, but only the first whip gets the whipcrack +1A.<sup><Tooltip content="err49">✏️</Tooltip></sup>

name: Cathayan Longsword
flavour: Prized indeed are blades of Ithilmar forged by Elves. Even more masterful are the arms crafted by swordsmiths in Cathay. Known as a Jintachi blade among Estalian merchants, the Cathayan longsword is a deadly crown jewel in the hands of a skilled fighter. Gold alone is never enough to acquire such a weapon. Jintachi are heirlooms and highly coveted. The few still smithed are gifted only in reward for some heroic deed that is done in the kingdoms to the east.

specialRules.ruleAbbreviated: +1 Initiative and +1 Weapon Skill
specialRules.ruleFull: Attacks made with a Cathayan longsword give the bearer +1 Initiative and +1 Weapon Skill.

specialRules.ruleAbbreviated: An axe has an extra save modifier of -1
specialRules.ruleFull: An axe has an extra save modifier of -1, so a model with Strength 4 using an axe has a -2 save modifier when he hits an opponent in hand-to-hand combat.

name: Censer
flavour: The censer is a hollow spiked metal ball attached to a long chain and is swung like a flail. A plague infested shard of warpstone burns inside the ball and emits pestilential fumes that nauseate the opponents and may turn the wielder of the censer into a difficult target to shoot at.

specialRules.ruleAbbreviated: +2 Strength only first turn
specialRules.ruleFull: The +2 Strength bonus applies only to the first turn of hand to hand combat.

specialRules.ruleAbbreviated: Requires two hands to wield
specialRules.ruleFull: A censer requires two hands to be used and the wielder cannot use a shield, buckler or additional weapon in close combat.

specialRules.ruleAbbreviated: Toughness test for extra wound
specialRules.ruleFull: A model hit by the censer must take a Toughness test. Roll a D6. If the result is higher than the Toughness of the model taking the test, he will suffer an automatic wound in addition to the censer hit. A result of 6 always inflicts a wound. Also the model wielding the censer must take the test and will suffer a wound with a result of 6. Models of undead and possessed are immune to the fog of death and do not take the test.
